name: New York
agency: New York Department of State, Division of Corporations
address: One Commerce Plaza, 99 Washington Avenue, Albany, NY 12231-0001

llc.formName: Articles of Dissolution of Domestic Limited Liability Companies
steps: Vote to dissolve according to your operating agreement or by majority vote of members
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File final federal and New York State tax returns
Obtain Consent to Dissolution from the NYS Department of Taxation and Finance (required)
File Articles of Dissolution (Form DOS-1366-f) with the Department of State ($60 fee)
Include the Consent to Dissolution from the Department of Taxation and Finance with your filing
Cancel any DBAs, licenses, and permits
llc.notes: New York requires a Consent to Dissolution from the Department of Taxation and Finance before the Department of State will accept your Articles of Dissolution. This consent will not be issued until all tax returns have been filed and all tax liabilities paid.

corporation.formName: Certificate of Dissolution of Domestic Business Corporations
steps: Board of directors adopts a plan of dissolution
Shareholders approve the dissolution (two-thirds of outstanding shares required)
Wind up business affairs, notify creditors, and settle all obligations
File final federal and New York State tax returns (Form CT-3)
Obtain Consent to Dissolution (Form TR-960) from the NYS Department of Taxation and Finance
File Certificate of Dissolution with the Department of State ($60 fee)
Attach the Consent to Dissolution from the Department of Taxation and Finance
Distribute remaining assets to shareholders after all debts are paid
corporation.notes: Tax consent is mandatory. The Department of Taxation and Finance will not issue consent until all franchise tax returns are filed and all taxes paid. Follow instructions at tax.ny.gov/bus/doingbus/tr125.htm for the voluntary dissolution process.

tax.taxAgency: New York State Department of Taxation and Finance
tax.notes: Tax consent to dissolution is mandatory. Corporations must file a final CT-3 franchise tax return and mark the 'Final return' box. The Department of Taxation and Finance issues a Consent to Dissolution (Form TR-960) only after all returns are filed and taxes paid. This consent must be attached to the dissolution filing with the Department of State. Instructions available at tax.ny.gov/bus/doingbus/tr125.htm.

timeline.waitingPeriods: Tax consent process can take several weeks depending on filing history

requirements.employeeNotification: Must comply with New York WARN Act (requires 90 days notice for mass layoffs of 25+ employees). Provide final paychecks by regular payday.
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify all known creditors and claimants in writing. Publish notice for unknown claimants.
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is finalized and all pending matters are resolved
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Corporation continues to exist for purpose of winding up after dissolution is filed
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLCs require majority vote of members unless operating agreement specifies otherwise. Corporations require board resolution and two-thirds shareholder approval of outstanding shares.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
